attractions.name: Academy of Newark Building
attractions.description: The Academy of Newark Building is a historic Greek Revival structure built in 1843 to house the Academy of Newark, a predecessor to the University of Delaware. Over its long history, the building has served as a high school and a town library before being restored for university administrative use. Today, it is listed on the National Register of Historic Places and serves as a prominent architectural landmark on the university's campus. (University of Delaware, Wikipedia))
attractions.hours: Monday - Friday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Offices); Academy Square park is accessible daily. (University of Delaware, City of Newark)
attractions.days_open: Monday - Friday (Offices); Daily (Academy Square Park) (University of Delaware)
attractions.admission: Free (University of Delaware)
highlights: 'National Register of Historic Places listing (since 1976) ([Wikipedia)', 'Historic Greek Revival architecture featuring a distinctive cupola (National Park Service)', 'Historical successor to the original Newark Academy founded in 1743 (UD Review)', 'Former location of the first Newark High School and the Newark Town Library (Wikipedia))', 'Current headquarters for the University of Delaware’s Office of Communications and Marketing (University of Delaware)', 'Adjacent to Academy Square, a landscaped public park with monuments (National Park Service)']
attractions.tips: The building is primarily used for university administration, but the historic exterior and the adjacent Academy Square park are open to the public for viewing. Visitors should look for public parking on Main Street, as the adjacent Lot 21 is a gated university lot. (University of Delaware)
attractions.nearby: University of Delaware Campus, Old College Gallery, Deer Park Tavern, Newark Main Street Shopping District (Visit Wilmington)

attractions.name: Ashland Nature Center
attractions.description: Ashland Nature Center serves as the headquarters of the Delaware Nature Society, featuring 130 acres of diverse habitats including woodlands, meadows, and marshlands. The site is a key educational hub offering four miles of trails, a seasonal butterfly house, and year-round nature programs for all ages. It is situated within the Red Clay Valley Scenic Byway and is recognized as an Important Bird Area. Official Website, Tripadvisor
attractions.hours: Trails & Grounds: Sunrise to Sunset daily. Visitor Center (Apr-Oct): Mon-Fri 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Sat-Sun 10:00 AM - 3:00 PM. (Note: Closed on Easter Sunday, Christmas Eve, Christmas, and New Year's Day) Official Website
attractions.days_open: Trails & Grounds: Daily (Year-round); Visitor Center: Monday–Sunday (Apr–Oct) Official Website
attractions.admission: Free for both members and non-members Official Website
highlights: 'Explore four miles of interpretive, pet-friendly trails including the Succession and Floodplain trails [Official Website', 'Visit the seasonal Butterfly House and surrounding pollinator gardens Official Website', 'Witness the Ashland Hawk Watch on Hawk Watch Hill during fall migration Delaware Nature Society', 'Observe local birdlife from the specialized Bird Blind and Hummingbird Haven Official Website', 'Discover historical features such as the nearby Ashland Covered Bridge The Family Corgi', 'Participate in year-round educational activities, school programs, and summer camps Official Website']
attractions.tips: Wear bug spray for ticks and mosquitoes, especially in wooded areas. Check for seasonal trail closures, particularly during the one-month hunting season. Dogs are welcome on leashed trails, and a pet waste station is available near the parking lot. The Family Corgi, Tripadvisor
attractions.nearby: Mt. Cuba Center, Winterthur Museum, Garden & Library, Auburn Valley State Park, Marshall Steam Museum, Coverdale Farm Preserve Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Aunt Margaret's Antique Mall, LLC
attractions.description: Aunt Margaret’s Antique Mall is a historic shopping destination located in downtown Newark, DE, housed in a unique building that previously served as a church and a police station. The mall features two floors filled with an exceptional variety of antiques, primitives, vintage collectibles, and memorabilia from numerous individual dealers. It offers a constantly changing inventory that blends the old and new, making it a popular spot for treasure hunters and those seeking unique gifts.

(Official Website)']
attractions.tips: Visitors are encouraged to browse both the main floor and the basement for hidden finds, and should note that aisles can be narrow, so leaving large bags in the car is recommended. Since the mall hosts many individual vendors, the staff may not be familiar with every specific item's location, so enjoy the process of 'hunting' through the various booths. All sales are final. (Tripadvisor, Official Website)

attractions.name: Bob Hannah Stadium
attractions.description: Bob Hannah Stadium is the official home of the University of Delaware Blue Hens baseball team. Originally opened in 1966 as Delaware Diamond, the stadium was renamed in 2000 to honor Hall of Fame coach Bob Hannah and features a fully artificial playing surface with a capacity of approximately 1,300. (University of Delaware Athletics, Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Brandywine Creek State Park Official Site
attractions.description: Brandywine Creek State Park is a 933-acre public recreation area in northern Delaware's Piedmont region, known for its rolling meadows and historic blue gneiss stone walls. Originally a du Pont family dairy farm, the park features four nature preserves, including Delaware's first old-growth forest and freshwater marsh. Official Site, Wikipedia

attractions.name: Cape Henlopen State Park
attractions.description: Cape Henlopen State Park is a 5,000-acre natural wonder situated where the Delaware Bay meets the Atlantic Ocean. It offers a diverse coastal ecosystem featuring pristine beaches, maritime forests, and historic World War II landmarks like Fort Miles. (Delaware State Parks, Tripadvisor)
attractions.hours: Park: 8:00 AM – Sunset daily (Fishing Pier area open 24 hours). Seaside Nature Center: 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM (Nov–March, Wed–Sun) or 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Mid-June–Labor Day, Daily). (SouthDel Sidekick, Delawonder)
attractions.admission: Daily entry fees (March 1 – November 30): $10 for Delaware-registered vehicles, $20 for out-of-state vehicles. Free for Delaware firefighters and EMTs. Annual passes are available for $50 (in-state) and $100 (out-of-state). (State of Delaware, Delaware State Parks)
highlights: 'The Point: Where the Delaware Bay and Atlantic Ocean meet, offering scenic views and wildlife watching. ([Thompson Island)', 'Fort Miles Museum & Historical Area: A WWII coastal defense site featuring underground bunkers and observation towers. (Delaware State Parks)', 'Seaside Nature Center: Features a 5,000-gallon touch tank and exhibits on local marine life. (Thompson Island)', 'Walking Dunes Trail: A 2.6-mile loop through impressive sand dunes and diverse habitats. (Thompson Island)', 'Gordons Pond Trail: A scenic trail with an elevated boardwalk over salt marshes. (Thompson Island)', "Borrow-a-Bike Program: Free bicycle rentals for exploring the park's designated trails. (Thompson Island)"]
attractions.tips: Make campground reservations well in advance, especially for summer. Use the Borrow-a-Bike program to explore the 16-mile Regional Loop. Visit the WWII Observation Tower for panoramic views of the park. (Thompson Island)
attractions.nearby: Rehoboth Beach, Lewes-Rehoboth Canal, Delaware Breakwater Lighthouse, Junction and Breakwater Trail. (Tripadvisor, Thompson Island)

attractions.name: City of Lewes (The First Town in the First State)
attractions.description: Lewes is a historic seaside city located where the Delaware Bay and the Atlantic Ocean meet in Sussex County. Founded by the Dutch in 1631, it is famously known as the "First Town in the First State" and features a preserved historic district, maritime heritage, and scenic coastal views. Lewes Official Website, Visit Delaware

attractions.tips: Visit during the spring or fall to enjoy the historic charm without summer crowds. Bring a bicycle to ride the Junction and Breakwater Trail that connects to Rehoboth Beach. attractions.name: City of Newark Reservoir
attractions.description: The City of Newark Reservoir is a man-made 317-million-gallon water supply facility that also serves as a popular public recreation area. Perched atop a high hill, it features a 1.2-mile paved multi-use trail circling the water, offering panoramic views of the surrounding Newark landscape and the Delaware Memorial Bridge in the distance. It is a favored spot for walking, jogging, and birdwatching in a tranquil setting.
attractions.address: 240 Old Paper Mill Rd, Newark, DE 19711
attractions.hours: Sunrise to Sunset
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: ['1.2-mile paved multi-use trail circling the reservoir', 'Panoramic views of Newark and the Delaware Memorial Bridge', '317 million gallon capacity water supply facility', 'Direct trail connections to William M. Redd, Jr. Park and White Clay Creek State Park', 'Popular location for sunset viewing and birdwatching (eagles and herons)']
attractions.tips: Dress in layers as the hilltop can be very windy and exposed. Be prepared for a steady incline walk from the parking lot to reach the reservoir level. Ensure all valuables are secured or removed from your vehicle, as thefts have been reported in the parking area. The trail is open and visible, making it a safe option for solo visitors.

attractions.name: David and Louise Roselle Center for the Arts (CFA)
attractions.description: The Roselle Center for the Arts is a premier performing arts facility at the University of Delaware, housing multiple venues for music and theatre. It serves as the primary home for the UD School of Music and the Resident Ensemble Players professional theatre company. The center features the 400-seat Thompson Theatre, the intimate Gore Recital Hall, and the versatile Puglisi Orchestra Hall. Recognized as one of the most amazing campus theatres, it hosts a diverse array of concerts, plays, and university events throughout the year.

attractions.tips: Arrive at least 30 minutes before performances as doors typically open one hour prior. Use the attached CFA Parking Garage (Passport App zone 1663) for convenient access. attractions.name: Deer Park Tavern
attractions.category: Restaurant, Bar, Historic Landmark Official Website, Wikipedia
attractions.description: Deer Park Tavern is a historic landmark and vibrant social hub located in the heart of Newark, Delaware, serving as a popular spot for University of Delaware students and locals alike. Built in 1851 on the site of the former St. Patrick's Inn, the Greek Revival-style brick building is famous for its storied past, including a legendary curse by Edgar Allan Poe and its listing on the National Register of Historic Places. Today, it offers a diverse menu of American fare, a massive Bloody Mary bar during weekend brunch, and a lively atmosphere with live music and outdoor decks. Official Website, UD Review

attractions.description: The Delaware Art Museum is a premier cultural institution best known for its world-class collection of British Pre-Raphaelite art and American illustration. Founded in 1912 to honor Wilmington artist Howard Pyle, the museum now houses more than 12,000 objects across nine galleries and a nine-acre outdoor sculpture garden. Wikipedia, Delaware Art Museum

It is also home to the Iron Hill Museum, which highlights the area's rich history as a former iron mining site and a location of Revolutionary War activity (New Castle County, Wikipedia)).

attractions.name: Old Swedes Historic Site (Holy Trinity Church)
attractions.description: Old Swedes Historic Site features the Holy Trinity (Old Swedes) Church, a National Historic Landmark consecrated in 1699 and considered the oldest church in the U.S. still used for worship as originally built. The site also encompasses a historic burial ground dating back to 1638 and the Hendrickson House, a 17th-century Swedish stone farmhouse that serves as a museum and research center. It stands as a significant remnant of the New Sweden Colony, documenting nearly four centuries of American history. Old Swedes Historic Site, National Park Service

attractions.name: Winterthur Museum, Garden & Library
attractions.description: Winterthur is the premier museum of American decorative arts, featuring an unparalleled collection of nearly 90,000 objects in the 175-room former home of Henry Francis du Pont. The estate encompasses 1,000 acres of protected rolling hills, meadows, and woodlands, including a world-renowned 60-acre naturalistic garden designed by du Pont himself. It also serves as a major center for American art and culture through its extensive research library and graduate programs About Winterthur, Museum Overview
attractions.hours: Garden and Estate: Tue–Sun 10:00 am–5:00 pm; House Tours: Tue–Sun 10:00 am–3:30 pm; Museum Store: Tue–Sun 10:00 am–5:00 pm; Library: Mon–Fri 9:00 am–4:00 pm (Special Collections by appointment 10:00 am–4:00 pm) Hours & Accessibility
attractions.admission: 2026 General Admission: $27 Adult, $25 Senior (62+), $25 Student (13+ with ID), $10 Child (3–12), Infant (under 2) Free. Yuletide Admission: $31 Adult, $29 Senior/Student, $10 Child. Museums for All: $2 (with SNAP EBT card) Admission Packages
highlights: ['175-room mansion featuring the most significant collection of American decorative arts in the world.', '60-acre naturalistic garden including the Sundial Garden, Magnolia Bend, and Azalea Woods.', 'Enchanted Woods, a unique three-acre award-winning children’s garden with fairy-tale features.', 'Winterthur Library, a world-class research facility for the study of American art and culture.', 'Garden Tram Tour, a 30-minute narrated highlights tour of the estate’s blooming landscapes.', 'Campbell’s Collection of Soup Tureens, a unique exhibit of historic decorative vessels.']
attractions.tips: General admission tickets are valid for two consecutive days, allowing ample time to explore both the house and the extensive grounds. Wear comfortable walking shoes as the estate covers 1,000 acres with over 25 miles of trails. Special collections in the library require an appointment at least 24 hours in advance Plan Your Visit

attractions.name: Brandywine Park
attractions.description: Brandywine Park is the largest urban park in Delaware, winding alongside the Brandywine River in the heart of Wilmington. Established in 1886 and designed in consultation with Frederick Law Olmsted, it features formal gardens, scenic trails, and historical monuments. The park serves as a hub for recreation, housing the Brandywine Zoo and hosting various community events throughout the year. Friends of Wilmington Parks, Wikipedia

attractions.tips: Visit in late spring to see the roses and cherry blossoms in full bloom. The park is dog-friendly, making it a popular spot for pet owners. If visiting the zoo, check their specific schedule as admission ends before the park closes. Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Christiana Mall
attractions.description: Christiana Mall is the largest shopping mall in Delaware and a major retail destination in the Mid-Atlantic region. This one-level, enclosed super-regional mall features over 170 stores, offering a tax-free shopping experience that attracts millions of visitors annually. It is anchored by major retailers such as Macy's, JCPenney, and Target, and includes a diverse range of dining and entertainment options.
attractions.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM - 8:00 PM, Tuesday: 10:00 AM - 8:00 PM, Wednesday: 10:00 AM - 8:00 PM, Thursday: 10:00 AM - 8:00 PM, Friday: 10:00 AM - 8:00 PM, Saturday: 10:00 AM - 8:00 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM - 6:00 PM
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: ['Over 170 tax-free shops including Apple, Nordstrom, and Lululemon Athletica.', "Major anchor stores: Macy's, JCPenney, Target, and Barnes & Noble.", 'Extensive dining options ranging from a large food court to sit-down restaurants like The Cheesecake Factory.', 'On-site entertainment including a 12-screen Cinemark theater.', 'Conveniently located at the intersection of I-95 and DE Route 1 for easy regional access.', 'Mall walking program available daily, starting as early as 7:00 AM on weekdays.']
attractions.tips: Take advantage of Delaware's tax-free shopping, but be prepared for larger crowds on weekends. Note the mall's parental guidance policy, which requires visitors under 18 to be accompanied by an adult on Friday and Saturday after 3:00 PM. Ample free parking is available, though preferred paid parking is also an option. Mall walkers are welcome starting at 7:00 AM Monday through Saturday.

attractions.name: Fort Delaware State Park (Official Website)
attractions.description: Fort Delaware State Park is a historic Civil War-era fortress situated on Pea Patch Island in the middle of the Delaware River. It once served as a camp for Confederate prisoners of war and a key military defense post. Today, the park offers immersive living history programs where costumed interpreters and daily cannon firings recreate the experience of 19th-century military life. (Official Website, American Battlefield Trust)
attractions.address: 45 Clinton Street, Delaware City, DE 19706 (Official Website)
attractions.hours: Ticket Office: 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM; Island Hours: 10:00 AM - 4:30 PM. Seasonal Schedule: Summer (Jun 11 - Sep 1): Wed-Sun, 10:00 AM - 4:30 PM; Spring (Apr 26 - Jun 8) & Fall (Sep 6 - Sep 28): Sat-Sun, 10:00 AM - 4:30 PM. Open Memorial Day, Juneteenth, July 4, and Labor Day. (Official Website)
attractions.days_open: Wednesday through Sunday (Summer Season); Saturdays and Sundays (Spring and Fall Seasons) (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Adults (13+): $15, Children (2-12): $8, Children (<2): Free, Seniors (62+) and Military: $14 (Official Website)
highlights: 'Immersive living history programs with costumed interpreters ([Official Website)', 'Daily demonstrations of the 8-inch Columbiad cannon firing (Official Website)', 'Extensive bird-watching opportunities for herons, egrets, and ibises at the nature preserve (Official Website)', 'Guided and self-guided tours of the restored Civil War fortress (Official Website)', 'Scenic ferry transportation to the island from Delaware City or New Jersey (Tripadvisor)', "Historical exhibits featuring artifacts from the island's past (StateParks.com)"]
attractions.tips: Be sure to wear closed-toe shoes as the terrain on the island is uneven. Advance ferry tickets are highly recommended, especially on weekends and holidays. Visitors under the age of 16 must be accompanied by an adult 18 or older. Private boats are not permitted to dock on Pea Patch Island. (Official Website)

The park features scenic paved and wooded trails, modern inclusive playgrounds, and specialized facilities like a skate park designed by Tony Hawk. New Castle County, Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Jessop's Tavern & Colonial Restaurant
attractions.description: Jessop's Tavern is a historic colonial-themed restaurant housed in a building dating back to 1674. It specializes in American, English, Dutch, and Swedish fare reflective of the region's heritage, and is renowned for its extensive collection of over 300 Belgian beers. Guests can enjoy a unique dining experience featuring servers in period costumes and traditional pewter dishware.
attractions.hours: Monday - Thursday: 11:30 AM - 9:00 PM; Friday - Saturday: 11:30 AM - 10:00 PM; Sunday: 11:30 AM - 8:00 PM
attractions.admission: Free (Restaurant/Bar)
highlights: ['Extensive selection of over 300 Belgian beers and 30 drafts', 'Historic 17th-century building originally built in 1674', 'Colonial-themed dining with staff in period costumes', "Specialty dishes like Oven Baked Pot Pies, Shepherd's Pie, and Dutch Pot Roast", 'Located in the heart of Historic New Castle near Battery Park', 'Unique homemade desserts including Dutch apple cakes with Woodside Farm Creamery ice cream']
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended, especially on weekends, due to the tavern's popularity. Be sure to explore the vast beer menu, which is often described as a book, and don't miss the homemade desserts like the Dutch apple cakes. The tavern is located in a walkable historic area, making it ideal for a combined visit with nearby waterfront parks.

attractions.name: New Castle Historic District (Historic Old New Castle)
attractions.description: Historic New Castle, overlooking the Delaware River, is the oldest continuously occupied town in the Delaware Valley and served as Delaware's first state capital. The town's historic district is a National Historic Landmark, featuring cobblestone streets and a remarkable collection of well-preserved colonial-era buildings dating back to the late 1600s. City of New Castle, Wikipedia

attractions.name: University of Delaware
attractions.description: The University of Delaware is a prestigious land-grant research institution known for its historic Georgian architecture and its flagship campus in Newark. It offers a wide variety of academic programs across ten colleges and serves as a vibrant center for research, innovation, and community engagement in an idyllic college town setting (University of Delaware, Wikipedia).

attractions.name: White Clay Creek State Park (Official Site)
attractions.description: Named for the clay once mined in the area, White Clay Creek State Park offers over 3,600 acres of scenic natural vistas and sparkling streams. It features more than 37 miles of trails for hiking, biking, and running, and is home to a nationally designated Wild and Scenic River. (Official Site)
attractions.address: 750 Thompson Station Road, Newark, DE 19711 (Official Site)
attractions.hours: Park: 8:00 AM – Sunset, daily; Park Office (Apr–Oct): 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM, daily; Park Office (Nov–Mar): 9:00 AM – 3:00 PM, Mon–Fri (Official Site)
attractions.admission: DE Registered Vehicle: $5/day; Non-DE Registered Vehicle: $10/day; Walking/Biking: Free (Official Site)
highlights: 'Over 37 miles of diverse trails for hiking, biking, and running ([Official Site)', 'The most heavily stocked trout fishing water in Delaware (Official Site)', 'Home to the Chambers House Nature Center and historical Mason-Dixon Line monuments (Official Site)', 'Scenic vistas and the federally protected Wild and Scenic White Clay Creek (Official Site)', 'Accessible facilities including fishing docks and track chairs for trail exploration (Official Site)']
attractions.tips: Entrance fees are charged from March 1 to November 3. For free parking, some visitors recommend parking at the connected White Clay Creek Preserve in Pennsylvania and hiking across the border. Water shoes are recommended for wading in the creek as rocks can be slippery. (Tripadvisor, SheBuysTravel)
